curses.textpad/assume_default_colors.name: assume_default_colors
curses.textpad/assume_default_colors.description: Allow use of default values for colors on terminals supporting this feature. Use this to support transparency in your application. Assign terminal default foreground/background colors to color number -1. So init_pair(x, COLOR_RED, -1) will initialize pair x as red on default background and init_pair(x, -1, COLOR_BLUE) will initialize pair x as default foreground on blue. Change the definition of the color-pair 0 to (fg, bg). Added in version 3.14.

curses.textpad/window/addch.name: addch
curses.textpad/window/addch.description: Paint character ch at (y, x) with attributes attr, overwriting any character previously painted at that location. By default, the character position and attributes are the current settings for the window object. Note Writing outside the window, subwindow, or pad raises a curses.error. Attempting to write to the lower right corner of a window, subwindow, or pad will cause an exception to be raised after the character is printed.

curses.textpad/window/addnstr.name: addnstr
curses.textpad/window/addnstr.description: Paint at most n characters of the character string str at (y, x) with attributes attr, overwriting anything previously on the display.

info.excerpt: Paint at most n characters of the character string str at (y, x) with attributes attr, overwriting anything previously on the display.

curses.textpad/window/addstr.name: addstr
curses.textpad/window/addstr.description: Paint the character string str at (y, x) with attributes attr, overwriting anything previously on the display. Note Writing outside the window, subwindow, or pad raises curses.error. Attempting to write to the lower right corner of a window, subwindow, or pad will cause an exception to be raised after the string is printed. A bug in ncurses, the backend for this Python module, can cause SegFaults when resizing windows. This is fixed in ncurses-6.1-20190511. If you are stuck with an earlier nc...

curses.textpad/window/attroff.name: attroff
curses.textpad/window/attroff.description: Remove attribute attr from the “background” set applied to all writes to the current window.

info.excerpt: Remove attribute attr from the “background” set applied to all writes to the current window.

curses.textpad/window/attron.name: attron
curses.textpad/window/attron.description: Add attribute attr to the “background” set applied to all writes to the current window.

info.excerpt: Add attribute attr to the “background” set applied to all writes to the current window.

curses.textpad/window/attrset.name: attrset
curses.textpad/window/attrset.description: Set the “background” set of attributes to attr. This set is initially 0 (no attributes).

info.excerpt: Set the “background” set of attributes to attr. This set is initially 0 (no attributes).
